title: Result - Portugal
content:
Welcome to Portugal, a sunny gem on Europe’s southwest coast, including the stunning Madeira and Azores archipelagos. With 3,000 hours of sunshine yearly and 850 kilometres of Atlantic coastline, Portugal is perfect for water sports like surfing, windsurfing and kitesurfing.
Known for its warm hospitality, Portugal boasts vibrant cities rich in culture, music, arts and cuisine. Explore national parks, ancient art sites, castles and historical villages, or attend year-round festivals. A leader in renewable energy and technology, Portugal attracts global investment while excelling in traditional products like fine wines and olive oils, blending innovation and heritage beautifully.

## Unique food tradition
Caldo verde (green broth): a traditional potato-based soup featuring collard greens and sausage.
Portugal has a growing job market, particularly in sectors such as technology, tourism and renewable energy.

### Local legend or myth
The Phantom Ship of São João Baptista: The legend dates back to 16th century, when a Portuguese ship vanished and sightings of a ghostly vessel resembling it began to appear near the Viana do Castelo coast, often before storms or disasters.

### Language of the Land
Portuguese is the fifth most widely spoken languages in the world.
kilometres long, the Vasco da Gama Bridge in Lisbon is Europe’s longest bridge over an expanse of water.
